title: Preserve extraction readiness across cached source reuse

title: Migrate Whetstone from Python scripts to a Rust binary
description: Port Whetstone from a Python-script prototype into a standalone Rust CLI while preserving the validated workflow, state semantics, cache behavior, doctor/status UX, and real-repo usefulness on projects like Splinter. Treat the current Python implementation as the executable spec, but make the Rust binary the primary product and distribution target.
acceptance_criteria: A standalone Rust whetstone binary can run doctor/status/detect-deps/resolve-sources on real repos without requiring Python; state/cache/workflow semantics are preserved or explicitly versioned; performance is at least as good as the current validated Python implementation; packaging and docs support binary-first installation; Python paths are deprecated only after parity is demonstrated.

title: Define Rust migration architecture and compatibility policy
description: Establish the target Rust architecture, migration sequencing, compatibility guarantees, and boundaries between deterministic binary behavior and agent-mediated extraction work.
acceptance_criteria: The repo has an explicit migration contract covering crate layout, state/versioning policy, command compatibility, and what remains agent-mediated versus Rust-implemented.

title: Define target Rust workspace and crate boundaries

acceptance_criteria: A clear crate map exists and each major responsibility has an intended Rust home before implementation begins.

title: Define command contract compatibility policy
description: Decide which current JSON outputs and behaviors must remain compatible and where versioned breaks are acceptable.
acceptance_criteria: The team has an explicit compatibility matrix for doctor, status, detect-deps, resolve-sources, and later generate commands.

title: Define state schema migration policy
description: Decide whether existing whetstone/.state artifacts are read directly, versioned in place, or migrated by tooling.
acceptance_criteria: There is a documented strategy for reading, migrating, and versioning persisted state without corrupting existing projects.

title: Define deterministic versus agent-mediated ownership
description: Clarify which responsibilities belong in the Rust binary and which remain agent judgment loops.
acceptance_criteria: No significant workflow step is left ambiguous between binary logic and agent behavior.

title: Build parity and regression validation between Python and Rust
description: Create a deliberate parity harness so Rust replacements are measured against the current Python behavior before Python is deprecated.
acceptance_criteria: The team can compare outputs, state transitions, and performance between Python and Rust implementations for key commands and representative repos.

title: Create fixture parity harness for command outputs
description: Build tests or comparison tooling that checks Rust command outputs against expected contracts and relevant Python baselines.
acceptance_criteria: Parity regressions are visible and reproducible on fixture repositories.

title: Benchmark cold warm and incremental performance on Splinter
description: Measure Rust doctor/status/detect-deps/resolve-sources against the current Python benchmarks on Splinter or similar repos.
acceptance_criteria: The migration has real performance evidence, not just assumed speed gains.

title: Validate cache resume and extraction-ready semantics end-to-end
description: Ensure Rust preserves the hard-won correctness around cache reuse, state-only repos, extraction readiness, and fast-first continuation.
acceptance_criteria: Rust passes regression scenarios that previously produced correctness bugs in the Python pipeline.

title: Define release readiness gate for Rust parity
description: Set explicit criteria for when a Rust command is ready to replace its Python counterpart in default docs and workflows.
acceptance_criteria: Replacement decisions are based on measured parity and readiness criteria rather than intuition.

title: Migrate docs agent context and contributor workflows to Rust-first

acceptance_criteria: All major docs and agent instructions describe a Rust-first workflow and avoid implying Python script paths are the main user story.

title: Rewrite quick start around binary install and external project targeting
description: Update user-facing docs so the default usage is installing the Rust binary and pointing it at another repo like Splinter.
acceptance_criteria: A new user sees binary-first usage rather than python scripts as the main adoption path.

title: Update agent context files for Rust-first workflows

title: Document transitional Python compatibility expectations

title: Mine Claude Code conversation transcripts for style patterns
description: Parse Claude Code .jsonl transcript files at ~/.claude/projects/{project}/. Extract user messages (type=user, message.role=user, message.content as string). Filter for style signals: directive phrases ('always use', 'prefer X over Y', 'don't use', 'make sure you', 'never do'), style keywords ('format', 'style', 'convention', 'naming'), correction patterns ('that's not how we do it', 'we use X here'), version-specific ('use the new', 'latest API', 'deprecated'). Group by semantic similarity and frequency across sessions. A pattern in 3+ separate sessions is high confidence.
acceptance_criteria: Correctly parses JSONL transcript format. Extracts user text messages (handles both string and list content formats). Style signal detection finds real patterns. Session-level dedup works (same pattern in same session counted once). Handles missing/empty transcript directories gracefully.

title: Analyze git commit history for style-related changes

Use subprocess to call git log and git diff.
acceptance_criteria: Commit message analysis finds style-related commits. Diff analysis detects repeated mechanical patterns. Handles repos with no style commits gracefully. Respects --since flag for time-bounded analysis.

title: Fetch GitHub PR review comments for convention feedback

Group by reviewer + pattern (same reviewer giving same feedback = high signal). Extract the 'what to do instead' from the comment text. Gracefully skip if gh is not installed or not authenticated.
acceptance_criteria: Fetches PR comments when gh is available. Filters for style-relevant comments. Groups by reviewer+pattern. Skips gracefully when gh unavailable (no crash, just empty results for this source).

title: Pattern grouping, scoring, and strictness filters
description: Implement the aggregation and filtering layer: (1) Frequency floor — pattern must appear in 3+ separate sessions/commits, (2) Recency bias — last 30 days weighted 3x vs older, (3) Dedup against existing — skip if pattern matches an existing rule in whetstone/rules/ or linter config, (4) Actionability check — pattern must be expressible as a testable signal. Rank by composite score. Also implement --since-last-run (reads .last-run timestamp), --since, --quiet, --sources flags.

description: Carry more explicit metadata about how sources were chosen and how fresh they are.
acceptance_criteria: Source resolution output clearly indicates priority, provenance, and drift-relevant metadata.

title: Strengthen Python enforcement outputs
description: Keep Python as the reference-quality implementation with strong deterministic checks and clear mappings to native tooling.
acceptance_criteria: Python rules generate understandable pytest and Ruff outputs for common rule classes, with clear support boundaries documented.

title: Refine supported Python signal-to-enforcement mappings
description: Catalog the Python signal patterns Whetstone can enforce well and tighten generation around them.
acceptance_criteria: Common Python rule classes map cleanly to maintainable generated tests or lint overlays.

title: Improve generated Python artifact readability
description: Ensure generated tests and config fragments are maintainable and explainable by humans.
acceptance_criteria: Python outputs include enough structure and context to be debugged and trusted.

title: Raise TypeScript output to a useful baseline
description: Narrow TypeScript support to the rule classes Whetstone can enforce credibly and align outputs with existing ecosystem tools.
acceptance_criteria: TypeScript support is smaller but trustworthy, generated outputs are usable in normal repos, and unsupported cases fail honestly.

title: Define the supported TypeScript rule surface
description: Choose the TypeScript rule classes that Whetstone can enforce well with current tooling.
acceptance_criteria: The supported TypeScript scope is explicit and grounded in real enforcement backends.

title: Align TypeScript generation with Biome typescript-eslint and Semgrep
description: Map generated outputs to the strongest available lint and scanning surfaces instead of bespoke placeholders.
acceptance_criteria: Generated TypeScript outputs preferentially target credible backends and reduce scaffolding-only cases.

title: Raise Rust output to a useful baseline
description: Constrain Rust support to high-signal rule classes and align generation with credible Rust-native enforcement paths.
acceptance_criteria: Rust outputs handle a narrow, valuable set of rule types well, and unsupported cases are explicit.

title: Define the supported Rust rule surface
description: Select the Rust rule categories Whetstone can enforce credibly with cargo tests, Clippy, and pattern checks.
acceptance_criteria: The supported Rust scope is explicit and oriented around high-confidence checks.

title: Evaluate Dylint as the richer custom-lint backend
description: Assess whether Dylint should become the preferred path for custom Rust enforcement beyond Clippy mappings.
acceptance_criteria: The project has a documented decision on if and when Dylint should be adopted.

title: Improve rule quality model and provenance
description: Tighten schema expectations and freshness signals so every approved rule remains explainable and reviewable over time.
acceptance_criteria: Rules consistently carry provenance, confidence, examples, and stale-detection inputs, making low-quality rules easier to reject.

title: Tighten schema requirements for provenance and examples
description: Strengthen rule schema usage around source attribution, golden examples, and confidence metadata.
acceptance_criteria: Schema-backed artifacts enforce the trust signals Whetstone depends on.

title: Add doc-drift and stale-rule detection strategy
description: Define how source changes and content hashes should mark rules as needing review.
acceptance_criteria: Stale rules can be detected and surfaced consistently.

title: Build a better doctor and status experience
description: Make doctor and status the primary UX for understanding repo coverage, freshness, and next actions.
acceptance_criteria: First-run and repeat-run UX clearly communicate findings, enforceable opportunities, staleness, and recommended next steps.

title: Design report-style doctor output
description: Shape doctor into a concise but confidence-building report that explains what Whetstone found and what it can do next.
acceptance_criteria: Doctor output is understandable without reading implementation docs.

title: Improve status recommendations and freshness reporting
description: Make status outputs more actionable and less abstract, especially around drift and missing coverage.
acceptance_criteria: Status highlights concrete actions, not just metrics.

title: UX: PR comment for drift and recommendations
description: Optional PR comment mode that posts what changed upstream, which rules are impacted, and exact command to run next. Keep comments concise and idempotent (update existing bot comment). Success: reviewers can act without opening logs.
close_reason: All child tasks complete. PR comments fully integrated into action.yml and ci-check.py.

title: Add PR comment generation to ci-check.py
description: Extend ci-check.py to generate a markdown comment body with: drift summary, impacted rules, recommended next commands. Include an HTML marker comment for idempotent updates.
close_reason: PR comment generation implemented in ci-check.py --pr-comment. Includes HTML marker for idempotent updates, table of health dimensions, recommendations, and next command.

title: Add PR comment posting step to action.yml
description: Add a conditional step to action.yml that posts/updates a PR comment when github-token is provided and event is pull_request. Use actions/github-script for comment management.
close_reason: PR comment posting step added to action.yml using actions/github-script. Updates existing comment if found, creates new one otherwise.

title: References, Config Template, and Integration Testing
description: Create reference documents (rule-schema.yaml, extraction-prompt.md, signal-strategies.md), the whetstone.yaml.template config file, and perform end-to-end smoke testing of the full init -> extract -> generate workflow. These are the supporting materials that tie everything together.
acceptance_criteria: All reference files exist and are complete. whetstone.yaml.template covers all config options. End-to-end flow works: detect-deps -> resolve-sources -> detect-patterns -> (agent extracts rules) -> generate-agent-context -> generate-tests.
notes: BUILD ORDER: Start after ALL other epics are complete. This is the final integration pass.

title: Create references/rule-schema.yaml
description: Create the definitive rule YAML schema reference at references/rule-schema.yaml. Define all fields with types, constraints, and descriptions: id, severity (must/should/may), confidence (high/medium), category (migration/default/convention/breaking-change/semantic), description, source_url, approved, approved_at, signals (array with id, strategy, description, weight), golden_examples (array with code, verdict, reason), deterministic_pass_threshold, deterministic_fail_threshold, ai_eval (trigger, question, context_lines). This is the source of truth that SKILL.md and generate scripts reference.
acceptance_criteria: Schema covers all fields from planning/product-spec.md rule format. Types and constraints are explicit. Examples included for complex fields.

title: Create references/extraction-prompt.md
description: Extract the full extraction prompt into references/extraction-prompt.md as a standalone reference. This is the detailed version of what's summarized in SKILL.md — the complete prompt that the agent uses when analyzing dependency documentation. Includes: category definitions, signal decomposition instructions, golden example requirements, anti-noise filters, the 5-rule ceiling, and explicit rejection criteria.
acceptance_criteria: Prompt is complete and self-contained. An agent reading only this file could perform a correct extraction. Rejection criteria are explicit with examples.

title: Create references/signal-strategies.md
description: Create references/signal-strategies.md documenting the four signal types: ast (syntax tree checks), pattern (text/regex matching), lint_proxy (maps to existing linter rules), ai (LLM judgment, last resort). For each type: when to use it, how it maps to generated test code per language, weight semantics (required/strong/moderate), and examples. Include the signal decomposition example from the product spec (error messages -> 4 signals, 3 deterministic).
acceptance_criteria: All 4 signal strategies documented with examples. Weight semantics explained. Per-language mapping described. Decomposition example included.

title: Create assets/whetstone.yaml.template
description: Create the default whetstone.yaml config template that whetstone init generates. Sections: languages (auto-detected), sources (per dep with docs_url, llms_txt_url, content_hash, version), agents (list of formats to generate), trigger (mode, schedule, auto_detect_patterns), custom_sources (user-added URLs). Include comments explaining each field.
acceptance_criteria: Template covers all config options. Comments are clear and helpful. Valid YAML when filled in.

title: End-to-end smoke test of full workflow
description: Run the full workflow end-to-end on a real project: (1) detect-deps finds dependencies, (2) resolve-sources fetches docs, (3) detect-patterns finds style patterns, (4) agent applies extraction prompt and proposes rules, (5) rules saved to YAML, (6) generate-agent-context produces CLAUDE.md + AGENTS.md, (7) generate-tests produces test files + linter configs. Verify all outputs are well-formed. Fix any integration issues between scripts.
acceptance_criteria: Full workflow completes without errors on a test project. All generated files are syntactically valid. Scripts communicate correctly via JSON. No orphaned or broken references.

title: P0: Fix changed-only end-to-end semantics
description: The action.yml 'changed-only' input and ci-check.py --changed-only flag currently only toggle drift on/off. They don't actually scope downstream checks to only changed dependencies — status.py still evaluates all rules. Fix: propagate changed-only through the full check pipeline so CI runs measurably faster on incremental changes. Exit criteria: integration test proves changed-only produces a strict subset of full-scan output and runs faster.

title: Add --changed-only to status.py that scopes evaluation to drifted deps only
description: status.py currently evaluates all rule files regardless of whether changed-only is requested. Add a --changed-only flag that: (1) runs detect-deps --changed-only to get drifted dep list, (2) filters rule_files to only those matching drifted deps, (3) computes dimensions only for the filtered set. ci-check.py should pass this flag through. Acceptance: test proves status --changed-only output.dependencies_covered is a subset of full output.

title: Add integration test proving changed-only reduces scope
description: Create a test fixture with multiple rule files. Run status --changed-only and full status. Assert: (1) changed-only dependencies_covered <= full dependencies_covered, (2) changed-only rules_count <= full rules_count, (3) changed-only completes faster. Acceptance: test in test_script_contracts.py passes.

title: P2: Strengthen release quality gates
description: CI currently runs contract tests only. Add lint (ruff), type checking (mypy/pyright), security scanning, dependency pinning, and action linting. Exit criteria: CI fails on lint violations, type errors, and known vulnerabilities.

title: Add ruff linting to CI
description: Add ruff check to .github/workflows/ci.yml. Fix any existing violations in scripts/. Acceptance: CI fails on ruff violations; all current code passes.

title: Add type checking to CI
description: Add mypy or pyright to CI workflow. Add type annotations to public function signatures in all scripts where missing. Acceptance: CI type check passes; public APIs are typed.

title: Add actionlint and dependency pinning to CI
description: Add actionlint step to validate action.yml and workflow YAML. Pin pip install versions for reproducibility. Add a requirements.txt or pyproject.toml with pinned dev deps. Acceptance: CI validates action syntax; dependency versions are deterministic.

title: P2: Unify command UX surface
description: Docs alternate between 'whetstone doctor' and 'python3 scripts/doctor.py --project-dir .'. Ship a lightweight whetstone CLI wrapper that dispatches to scripts, making the canonical UX consistent. Exit criteria: 'whetstone doctor' works as a real command; script paths kept as documented alternative.

title: Create whetstone CLI entry point that dispatches to scripts
description: Create a whetstone.py (or __main__.py) entry point: 'python3 -m whetstone doctor' or 'whetstone doctor' via pip install. Maps subcommands to scripts: doctor->doctor.py, status->status.py, check->ci-check.py, init->detect-deps+resolve-sources, update->detect-deps --changed-only, generate->generate-*.py. Passes through all flags. Acceptance: 'python3 -m whetstone doctor --project-dir .' produces same output as 'python3 scripts/doctor.py --project-dir .'.

title: Update docs to use unified command syntax as primary
description: Update README.md and SKILL.md to use 'whetstone <cmd>' as the primary syntax. Keep 'python3 scripts/<name>.py' as documented alternative for development/debugging. Acceptance: README Quick Start uses whetstone CLI; script paths noted in a 'Development' section.

title: generate-tests.py — Test + Linter Config Generation
description: Create scripts/generate-tests.py — reads approved rules from whetstone/rules/*.yaml and generates native test files and linter configuration overlays. Python: pytest files using ast/re modules + ruff.whetstone.toml. TypeScript: vitest files using regex + biome.whetstone.json. Rust: cargo test files using string/regex matching + clippy.whetstone.toml. Each generated test file includes header comment with rule ID, source URL, and generation notice.
acceptance_criteria: Generates test files for all three languages. Tests use native testing frameworks (pytest/vitest/cargo test). Generated tests are syntactically valid. Linter config overlays are valid TOML/JSON. Each file has proper header comment.
notes: BUILD ORDER: Start after whetstone-obv (SKILL.md) is done. Can run in parallel with whetstone-16h.

title: Generate Python pytest files and ruff config overlay
description: Generate pytest test files from Python rules: (1) AST signal rules -> test files using Python ast module to walk source trees and check patterns, (2) Pattern signal rules -> test files using re module for regex matching, (3) lint_proxy signal rules -> ruff.whetstone.toml overlay with enabled rules. Also generate conftest.py with shared helpers (source file discovery, AST walking utilities). Output to whetstone/evals/python/.
acceptance_criteria: Generated pytest files are syntactically valid Python. AST-based tests correctly walk source trees. Pattern tests use proper regex. ruff.whetstone.toml is valid TOML. conftest.py provides useful shared fixtures.

title: Generate TypeScript vitest files and biome config overlay
description: Generate vitest test files from TypeScript rules: (1) AST signal rules -> .test.ts files using regex-based source analysis (full ts-morph deferred to Rust CLI), (2) Pattern signal rules -> .test.ts files with regex, (3) lint_proxy signal rules -> biome.whetstone.json overlay. Also generate setup.ts with shared test utilities. Output to whetstone/evals/typescript/.
acceptance_criteria: Generated vitest files are syntactically valid TypeScript. Tests correctly scan source files. biome.whetstone.json is valid JSON. setup.ts provides useful shared utilities.

title: Generate Rust cargo test files and clippy config overlay
description: Generate cargo test files from Rust rules: (1) AST signal rules -> tests/whetstone_*.rs files using string matching and regex (full syn analysis deferred to Rust CLI), (2) Pattern signal rules -> same with regex crate, (3) lint_proxy signal rules -> clippy configuration in clippy.whetstone.toml or Cargo.toml [lints.clippy] section. Output to whetstone/evals/rust/.
acceptance_criteria: Generated Rust test files are syntactically valid. Tests use standard #[test] attribute. clippy.whetstone.toml is valid TOML configuration.

title: Test file templating system and shared header generation
description: Implement a simple templating system for test file generation. Each generated file should include: (1) Header comment with rule ID, source URL, 'Generated by Whetstone -- do not edit manually', (2) Configurable source paths (where to scan for violations), (3) Consistent structure across languages. Use Python string templates or f-strings — no heavy template engine needed for MVP.
acceptance_criteria: All generated files have consistent headers. Source paths are configurable. Template system is easy to extend for new rule types.

title: P1: Close output-contract gaps
description: Some code paths don't include next_command or have inconsistent error shapes. Guarantee every JSON output from every script always has next_command, and every error response has both 'error' and 'next_command'. Exit criteria: contract test covers all output paths including edge cases.

title: Fix detect-patterns quiet+empty path missing next_command
description: detect-patterns.py:582 returns {'patterns': [], 'sources_analyzed': ...} without next_command when quiet mode is on and no patterns found. Add next_command to this path. Acceptance: running detect-patterns --quiet on a project with no patterns includes next_command in JSON output.

title: Add exhaustive output-contract tests for all edge paths
description: Extend TestErrorContracts to cover: (1) detect-patterns quiet+empty, (2) detect-patterns quiet+found, (3) resolve-sources with no deps on stdin, (4) ci-check error path. Every test asserts next_command is present and is a non-empty string. Acceptance: 4+ new tests pass.
